nodes.planningStatus: Existing draft; keep distinct from Payment Networks

nodes.planningStatus: Existing draft; not child of Gold

nodes.planningStatus: Existing draft; keep; Networks placement confirms coverage depth 2

edges.rationale: Bitcoin as Money belongs under Bitcoin because it supplies a foundation the Money route needs before narrower mechanisms or examples can make sense.
Digital Money belongs under Settlement because it is an earlier form or historical precursor that clarifies what later Money concepts inherit, adapt or reject.
Modern Fiat and Credit Systems belongs under Political Control because it shows the broader Money concept put into practice in a concrete system, tool or institutional setting.
Political Control belongs under Trust because it supplies a foundation the Money route needs before narrower mechanisms or examples can make sense.
Scarcity belongs under Bitcoin as Money because it supplies a foundation the Money route needs before narrower mechanisms or examples can make sense.
Settlement belongs under Bitcoin as Money because it explains a mechanism by which the Money route works, is constrained or becomes verifiable.
Custody belongs under Bitcoin as Money because it explains a mechanism by which the Money route works, is constrained or becomes verifiable.
Ledgers belongs under Bitcoin as Money because it explains a mechanism by which the Money route works, is constrained or becomes verifiable.
Trust belongs under Bitcoin as Money because it supplies a foundation the Money route needs before narrower mechanisms or examples can make sense.
Blockchain belongs under Ledgers because it shows the broader Money concept put into practice in a concrete system, tool or institutional setting.
Central Banks belongs under Modern Fiat and Credit Systems because it names an institution that administers, standardises or shapes the relevant Money system.
Paper Claims belongs under Custody because it is an earlier form or historical precursor that clarifies what later Money concepts inherit, adapt or reject.
Coinage belongs under Monetary Metals because it shows the broader Money concept put into practice in a concrete system, tool or institutional setting.
Monetary Metals belongs under Scarcity because it is an earlier form or historical precursor that clarifies what later Money concepts inherit, adapt or reject.
Social Accounting belongs under Ledgers because it is an earlier form or historical precursor that clarifies what later Money concepts inherit, adapt or reject.
Social Memory belongs under Social Accounting because it supplies a foundation the Money route needs before narrower mechanisms or examples can make sense.
Money Functions belongs under Bitcoin as Money because it supplies a foundation the Money route needs before narrower mechanisms or examples can make sense.
Unit of Account belongs under Money Functions because it is a concrete example that makes the broader Money category sourceable without replacing the category itself.
Medium of Exchange belongs under Money Functions because it is a concrete example that makes the broader Money category sourceable without replacing the category itself.
Store of Value belongs under Money Functions because it is a concrete example that makes the broader Money category sourceable without replacing the category itself.
Specialisation belongs under Social Accounting because it supplies a foundation the Money route needs before narrower mechanisms or examples can make sense.
Digital Payments belongs under Digital Money because it shows the broader Money concept put into practice in a concrete system, tool or institutional setting.
Banking belongs under Custody because it shows the broader Money concept put into practice in a concrete system, tool or institutional setting.

Gold belongs under Monetary Metals because it is a concrete example that makes the broader Money category sourceable without replacing the category itself.

alsoTouches.reason: Scarcity is not an energy-history parent.
Extraction cost is relevant but does not make Gold a proof-of-work ancestor.
The relation is real but too broad for a navigable parent edge.
Legal enforceability and paper promises overlap but should stay distinct.
Legal tender may affect trust without becoming a Trust child.
Digital cash is a historical branch; digital payments is a broader system.
Payment networks are broader than bank account rails.
Settlement infrastructure is related, not the whole network.
Topology can help resistance but does not guarantee it.
Public code can affect governance without becoming a state-power node.
Forks expose discretion around rules but are not a philosophy parent.
Verification changes trust assumptions without eliminating trust.
Legal ownership and practical control must remain distinct.
Settlement is one important form of finality.
Capital controls are a policy branch, not the definition.
Sanctions are a policy branch, not the definition.
War finance can pressure issuance and extraction without making seigniorage its parent.

nodes.rationale: The root from which the atlas works backwards.
nodes.risks: Do not frame earlier money as inevitably leading to Bitcoin.

nodes.rationale: Places Bitcoin among earlier electronic money attempts without collapsing them together.
nodes.risks: Do not treat all digital money as decentralised.

nodes.rationale: Names the monetary environment Bitcoin enters and critiques.
nodes.risks: Avoid anti-fiat slogans and one-country assumptions.

nodes.rationale: Central banking concentrates monetary policy, settlement and liquidity functions.
nodes.risks: Do not treat every central bank mandate as identical.

nodes.rationale: Transferable claims help bridge custody, banking and later credit money.
nodes.risks: Do not imply paper claims are the whole story of fiat money.

nodes.rationale: Coinage gives a historical route into marked monetary objects and state authority.
nodes.risks: Do not present coinage as universal or first.

nodes.rationale: Metals anchor many monetary histories without exhausting them.
nodes.risks: Gold must not be treated as containing all commodity money.

nodes.rationale: Obligations, memory and exchange predate formal money objects.
nodes.risks: Do not present barter as a universal first stage.

nodes.rationale: The direct energy-facing Bitcoin practice.
nodes.risks: Do not make mining the whole energy strand.

nodes.rationale: Mining depends on electrified computation and grid infrastructure.
nodes.risks: Avoid implying grids exist for mining.

nodes.rationale: Electricity is the carrier that makes modern computation and mining practical.
nodes.risks: Do not reduce energy history to electricity.

nodes.rationale: Steam gives a genuine industrial-energy predecessor.
nodes.risks: Do not revive Industrial Power as a vague catch-all node.

nodes.rationale: Earlier mechanical power shows energy systems before industrial heat engines.
nodes.risks: Avoid a simple ladder from water to steam to Bitcoin.

nodes.rationale: Work before machines gives a historical baseline for labour and power.
nodes.risks: Do not moralise work as proof-of-work ancestry.

nodes.rationale: Fire and food energy anchor the oldest energy transformations.
nodes.risks: Do not force exact origin dates.

nodes.rationale: Bitcoin's relay and node topology belong here.
nodes.risks: Do not equate peer-to-peer with automatic decentralisation.

nodes.rationale: Bitcoin depends on internet communication layers.
nodes.risks: Do not make "the internet" a single bubble.

nodes.rationale: Packet switching is a hinge behind internet communication.
nodes.risks: Avoid treating it as identical to Bitcoin relay.

nodes.rationale: Electrical communication predates packet networks.
nodes.risks: Keep distinct from payment networks.

nodes.rationale: Telegraphy is the historical hinge for long-distance electrical messaging.
nodes.risks: Do not reduce it to one device or inventor.

nodes.rationale: Pre-electrical networks show organised long-distance message movement.
nodes.risks: Avoid implying all networks decentralise over time.

nodes.rationale: Durable signs and signals predate formal communications networks.
nodes.risks: Do not invent exact origin dates.

nodes.rationale: Bitcoin is executed and checked across many machines.
nodes.risks: Do not imply Bitcoin invented distributed computation.

nodes.rationale: Digital computers make protocol execution ordinary.
nodes.risks: Avoid a hardware-only history.

nodes.rationale: Transistors anchor modern computation materially.
nodes.risks: Do not overfit hardware to Bitcoin mining.

nodes.rationale: Programmability explains rule-following machines before modern software.
nodes.risks: Avoid a single invention story.

nodes.rationale: Turing machines clarify computation as formal rule execution.
nodes.risks: Algorithms is too broad for a structural bubble here.

nodes.rationale: Boolean logic connects formal reasoning to digital circuits.
nodes.risks: Do not turn logic into a chronology.

nodes.rationale: Mechanical calculation provides a pre-digital calculating-machine anchor.
nodes.risks: Do not imply direct descent to Bitcoin validation.

nodes.rationale: Counting and records connect computation to older social memory.
nodes.risks: Do not duplicate the Money route.

nodes.rationale: Signatures prove authorisation in Bitcoin.
nodes.risks: Do not describe signatures as encryption.

nodes.rationale: Hashing links data, commitments and proof-of-work puzzles.
nodes.risks: Do not collapse hashing into secrecy.

nodes.rationale: Public-key systems explain proof without shared secrets.
nodes.risks: Avoid implying public-key systems alone create money.

nodes.rationale: Modern cryptography grew with attacks, analysis and standards.
nodes.risks: Do not make cryptanalysis a Bitcoin ancestor by itself.

nodes.rationale: Mechanical systems bridge classical ciphers and computer-era cryptography.
nodes.risks: Encryption is not central to Bitcoin's base protocol.

nodes.rationale: Older secrecy practices anchor the pre-computer history.
nodes.risks: Do not present secrecy as the main Bitcoin mechanism.

nodes.rationale: Contemporary rules shape access to banking, exchanges and settlement.
nodes.risks: Do not collapse regulation into opposition to Bitcoin.

nodes.rationale: State authority over units, taxes and payment law differs from the Money strand's Political Control node.
nodes.risks: Avoid duplicate use as a vague synonym for power.

nodes.rationale: Fiscal states connect taxation, debt and administrative capacity.
nodes.risks: Do not imply one European pathway is universal.

nodes.rationale: Public debt explains credit, war finance and fiscal credibility.
nodes.risks: Keep distinct from private credit.

nodes.rationale: War repeatedly pressures money, debt and taxation.
nodes.risks: Avoid treating war as the only driver of monetary change.

nodes.rationale: Armies and enforcement explain power behind tribute, law and taxation.
nodes.risks: Avoid flattening states into pure coercion.

nodes.rationale: Capacity explains the ability to tax, enforce and administer systems.
nodes.risks: Keep separate from legitimacy claims.

nodes.rationale: Tax obligations help explain units, payment law and state money.
nodes.risks: Do not claim taxes alone create money.

nodes.rationale: Coinage shows authority marking and standardising money objects.
nodes.risks: Keep distinct from all coinage.

nodes.rationale: Tribute gives an older route into obligation, power and extraction.
nodes.risks: Do not make tribute and taxation identical.

nodes.rationale: Law frames enforceable obligations, property and settlement.
nodes.risks: Avoid one legal tradition standing for all.

philosophy-and-time.title: Philosophy & Time Genealogy
philosophy-and-time.note: This strand uses an intellectual genealogy rather than strict chronology.

nodes.rationale: Protocol rules provide the concrete point of departure.
nodes.risks: Do not turn "rules" into "code settles every question".

nodes.rationale: Verification explains checking rather than trusting a claim.
nodes.risks: Do not imply all trust disappears.

nodes.rationale: Trust is reduced, shifted or formalised across routes.
nodes.risks: Avoid treating trust as a single substance.

nodes.rationale: Property explains claims of control, exclusion and transfer.
nodes.risks: Do not make property theory a Bitcoin proof.

nodes.rationale: Ownership narrows property into recognised control claims.
nodes.risks: Do not retain Possession as a separate structural bubble.

nodes.rationale: Value anchors the difference between price, utility and judgement.
nodes.risks: Avoid making value a promotional claim.

nodes.rationale: Exchange connects valuation to coordination between people.
nodes.risks: Do not make exchange equal monetary exchange only.

nodes.rationale: Saving links value across time without duplicating Store of Value.
nodes.risks: Avoid treating saving as endorsement of one monetary theory.

nodes.rationale: Time preference explains present and future valuation.
nodes.risks: Keep contested economic interpretations visible.

nodes.rationale: Common knowledge explains coordination around shared expectations.
nodes.risks: Do not keep Schelling Points as a structural bubble yet.

nodes.rationale: Shared truth frames agreement about records and rules.
nodes.risks: Do not over-philosophise technical finality.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Move to Deeper example under Bitcoin Mining or Energy Markets
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Too source-sensitive and claim-shaped for a structural bubble.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Move to Deeper example under Bitcoin Mining
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Useful energy-market example, not a core atlas node.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Merge into Costly Production
duplicateRegister.Rationale: The phrase is too abstract and risks duplicating proof-of-work cost language.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Merge into Steam Power, Electricity and Electrical Grids and Computation
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Too broad as a node; the historical spine now uses clearer anchors.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Move to Deeper under Mechanical Encryption, Classical Ciphers or Privacy
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Encryption is not central to Bitcoin's base protocol.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Move to Deeper under Open-Source Software
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Relevant to software practice, but weak as a structural Bitcoin route.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Merge into Payment Networks or Lightning payment-channel discussion
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Duplicates liquidity and network effects without a clear canonical concept.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Convert to preview-only example under Privacy and Censorship Resistance
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Important tool, but too specific for current depth-four structure.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Merge into Saving and Time Preference
duplicateRegister.Rationale: The concept repeats the time trade-off already covered there.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Move to Deeper under Common Knowledge
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Useful example, but too narrow for the first philosophy structure.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Move to Deeper under Payment Networks
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Concrete example of payment infrastructure, not a necessary node.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Merge into Banking Rails
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Account transfer mechanics belong inside the broader banking-rails node.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Merge into Ownership and Property Rights
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Physical control should be handled as a subtopic, not a separate bubble.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Use Full Nodes
duplicateRegister.Rationale: "Nodes" is too broad; Full Nodes is the Bitcoin-specific canonical concept.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Keep both with scoped meanings
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Payment Networks is the broader network concept; Banking Rails is bank-account infrastructure.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Merge into Banking Rails
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Transfers are an example inside the rails system.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Keep Finality as canonical
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Settlement finality and irreversibility become contextual explanations.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Keep distinct
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Political Control is the Money-route framing; Monetary Sovereignty is state authority over units, tax and payment law.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Keep Energy and Costly Production; merge Thermodynamic Cost
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Costly Production is the reusable mechanism; Thermodynamic Cost is wording, not a node.

duplicateRegister.Issue: Store of Value / Saving / Purchasing Power
duplicateRegister.Decision: Keep all three narrowly scoped
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Store of Value is a money function; Saving is a practice; Purchasing Power is an outcome measure.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Do not keep Gold Mining
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Gold Mining belongs in Deeper under Gold or Costly Production.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Category corrected to Practice
duplicateRegister.Rationale: It is a central-bank function/practice, not an institution.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Edge corrected
duplicateRegister.Rationale: Commodity Money branches from Monetary Metals, not from Gold.

duplicateRegister.Decision: Parent corrected
duplicateRegister.Rationale: These are not one simple consequence of Seigniorage; they need separate historical treatment.

entries.Issue: Store of Value / Saving / Purchasing Power
entries.Rationale: Store of Value is a money function; Saving is a practice; Purchasing Power is an outcome measure.
Gold Mining belongs in Deeper under Gold or Costly Production.
It is a central-bank function/practice, not an institution.
Commodity Money branches from Monetary Metals, not from Gold.
These are not one simple consequence of Seigniorage; they need separate historical treatment.

entries.Historical nodes added: Digital Money; Modern Fiat and Credit Systems; Coinage; Monetary Metals; Social Accounting; Banking; Specialisation
entries.Rationale: Repairs the money spine without reviving barter as a universal origin.

entries.Historical nodes added: Electrical Grids and Computation; Water and Wind Power; Animal and Human Work; Fire and Biological Energy
entries.Rationale: Replaces a mining-heavy route with genuine energy history.

entries.Historical nodes added: Internet Protocols; Telecommunications; Telegraphy; Postal and Messenger Networks; Writing and Signalling
entries.Rationale: Adds pre-internet communications history and treats Telegraphy as a hinge.

entries.Historical nodes added: Distributed Computing; Digital Computers; Transistors; Programmable Machines; Turing Machines; Boolean Logic; Counting and Record-Keeping
entries.Rationale: Adds a rule-execution and machine-history spine.
Separates secrecy history from Bitcoin's signature and hash mechanisms.

entries.Historical nodes added: Modern Regulatory Systems; Fiscal States; Public Debt; War Finance; Organised Coercion; State Capacity; Tribute; Law
entries.Rationale: Gives the strand its own state-power history instead of duplicating Money's Political Control branch.

entries.Historical nodes added: Bitcoin Rules; Verification; Value; Exchange; Subjective Valuation; Time; Rules and Discretion; Shared Truth
entries.Rationale: Converts the strand into an intellectual genealogy with concrete review targets.
Energy may illuminate scarcity claims but should not host Scarcity navigably.
Legal force can affect trust without becoming a Trust child.
The connection is real but too broad and politically loaded for a first navigable edge.
Energy markets are regulated, but they belong to Energy.
Both involve enforceability and claims, but neither contains the other.

entries.Decision: Keep out of Also touches until canonical status is approved
entries.Rationale: The term is relevant to mining and regulation but is not currently a canonical node in this plan.

entries.Decision: Use only as a Deeper example under Bitcoin Mining or future Energy Markets material
entries.Rationale: Demand response is an energy-market example, not a canonical atlas node for this implementation pass.

entries.Decision: Use only as a source-sensitive Deeper example
entries.Rationale: Stranded energy is claim-shaped and should not appear as a preview endpoint without a canonical proposal.

implementationBatches.title: Existing Money sourcing backlog
implementationBatches.rationale: Cleans up existing public Money nodes before adding new structure.
implementationBatches.gateRationale: Blocked until the batch nodes, incoming navigable edges, contextual placements and approved shared contexts are semantically reviewed.

implementationBatches.title: Core shared Bitcoin mechanisms
implementationBatches.rationale: Establishes the shared technical concepts needed by later strands.
implementationBatches.gateRationale: Blocked until the batch nodes, incoming navigable edges, contextual placements and approved shared contexts are semantically reviewed.

implementationBatches.title: Historical Networks spine
implementationBatches.rationale: Adds communications history before network mechanisms sprawl.
implementationBatches.gateRationale: Blocked until the batch nodes, incoming navigable edges, contextual placements and approved shared contexts are semantically reviewed.

implementationBatches.title: Historical Computation spine
implementationBatches.rationale: Builds rule-execution and machine history before adding detailed computation branches.
implementationBatches.gateRationale: Blocked until the batch nodes, incoming navigable edges, contextual placements and approved shared contexts are semantically reviewed.

implementationBatches.title: Government & Power spine
implementationBatches.rationale: Gives state power its own spine, separate from Money's Political Control framing.
implementationBatches.gateRationale: Blocked until the batch nodes, incoming navigable edges, contextual placements and approved shared contexts are semantically reviewed.

implementationBatches.title: Energy history spine
implementationBatches.rationale: Replaces the mining-heavy energy route with real energy history.
implementationBatches.gateRationale: Blocked until the batch nodes, incoming navigable edges, contextual placements and approved shared contexts are semantically reviewed.

unresolvedQuestions: Should Scarcity remain a Money-only navigable node with Energy as preview-only, or does it need a carefully scoped shared parent later?
Should Digital Money be a shared canonical node, or should Digital Cash carry most of the cryptography lineage?
Does Modern Fiat and Credit Systems need splitting before implementation?
Is Social Accounting too broad, or is it the right non-barter historical anchor?
Should Monetary Sovereignty be implemented before or after the existing Political Control copy is reviewed?
Should Philosophy & Time keep Time as a node, or only use it as metadata and Deeper context?
Which historical regions should anchor Coinage, Taxation, Tribute and Law without implying universal development?
definitionOfPlanningDone: the seven historical spines have been approved or amended
duplicate and merge decisions have been reviewed
approved shared candidates have approved parent rationales where they are navigable
Also touches relationships are confirmed as preview-only
temporal metadata has been copied into each bubble proposal
source categories have been converted into real source inventories
the first implementation batch has approved proposals
no route is kept merely to reach depth four
semantic validator warnings have been reviewed or resolved
Also touches endpoints are canonical nodes only
approved shared candidates, realised multi-parent nodes and multi-strand placements are reported separately
each implementation batch has an explicit gate status
batch-gated validation passes before any public implementation begins

approvedSharedCandidates.rationale: Payment finality, rail design and legal settlement all need the same canonical concept.
Control over assets links institutions, keys and ownership claims.
Trust is shifted between people, institutions, verification and rules.
Records of obligation, state and transaction history belong across routes.
Blockchain joins ledger structure, hash commitments, replication and propagation.
Central banks connect fiat systems, reserves and settlement infrastructure.
Legal settlement rules must stay narrow and source-led.
Proof of work is energy expenditure, a hash puzzle and a coordination mechanism.
Hashes are commitments and proof-of-work inputs.
Signatures connect spend authority with public verification.
Private keys connect secret control, custody and ownership risk.
Peer relay and distributed system design share the same architecture concept.
Full nodes check rules and participate in relay.
Ownership claims require legal, philosophical and custody framing.
Finality links settlement confidence, time and ledger reorganisation risk.
State authority over money needs both political and monetary framing.
Public-key proof explains non-custodial control without central accounts.
Digital money links monetary function, digital cash experiments and networked payments.

sharedAccountingSummary: The approved shared-candidate list is declared separately from realised multi-parent nodes and multi-strand placements.
